preview_text:
The model forecasts Argentina as a heavy favorite at 80%, leaving Jordan with a 4% chance of victory. The underdog's path to an upset is narrow, though not nonexistent within the uncertainty bounds of match simulation.

The win-draw-loss spread reflects a lopsided matchup. Argentina's dominance in the forecast means a Jordan win would rank as a considerable upset; a draw at 17% is the most plausible non-Argentina outcome, suggesting the match carries some competitive elasticity but tilts sharply in one direction.

Model and sportsbook consensus align closely on the Argentina win probability (80% vs 79%), diverging only marginally on the draw (17% vs 14%). The market leans slightly toward a decisive result, while the model assigns modestly higher draw probability. Both sources position this as a strongly favored Argentina performance. Polymarket prices show a 2-3 percentage-point tilt toward Argentina compared to the stripped book consensus, within typical variance across venues.
